preregistration: this file, committed before any generation (record-first). Owner spend approval given in-session 17-Jul and recorded on issue #214. The 13-Jul worktree campaign's 4/8 finding is a LEAD, not evidence (owner direction on the issue); this run is the canonical re-measurement with the current skill-bench harness.
hypothesis_l1: The current code-standards description leaves recoverable should-fire coverage on the table: a matched-protocol variant durably fires on more should-fire queries with no durably lost query and no durable false-positive raise. Falsified if no variant satisfies the adoption rule (null recorded, current description kept).
hypothesis_l2: Loading the code-standards SKILL.md body changes graded output quality on its 6 committed evals versus a bare arm. Direction unstated; KEEP/CUT per benchstats.keep_verdict (ADR 0025). A null is an equally valid outcome. (Grounding, not evidence: the 13-Jul lead measured +0.49.)
gate_or_optimization: gate (ADR 0062): go/no-go on shipping a description fix and on the skill's measured body value; not a powered optimization.

l1_protocol.queries: committed trigger-kit 8 should-fire + 8 should-not-fire (benchmarks/2026-07-07-toolkit-grid/trigger-kit/code-standards-queries.json), derived at run time
l1_protocol.durable_fire: a query durably fires when triggers/completed_runs >= 2/3; a timeout run is excluded from the denominator (null, never a False)
l1_protocol.variants: control (current live description) + at most 2 variants (descriptions.json, authored pre-run; hard cap per the #30 precedent)
l1_protocol.adoption_rule: adopt a variant only if (a) its durable should-fire count exceeds control's, (b) NO should-fire query control durably fires is durably lost, and (c) durable false-positives are not raised vs control. Otherwise record the null and keep the current description. Absolute rates are competitive-not-isolated (nested sessions inherit built-in skills) — only within-run A/B comparisons are read.

l2_protocol.evals: skills/code-standards/evals/evals.json (6 committed evals, self-contained prompts, 6 expectations each), mapped {id, prompt->task, expectations} at run time

arms.bare: hermetic claude -p sonnet, ALL tools denied, neutral empty cwd
arms.with: bare + --append-system-prompt = skills/code-standards/SKILL.md body, frontmatter stripped (the FULL loaded surface — no references dir exists, ADR 0019 item 4)

l2_protocol.graded_artifact_rule: the response text (tool-denied text tasks produce no files); artifact_check min 200 chars; failures are ERROR cells never quality 0 (#191); capture_symmetry gates grading

prescreen.rule: 1 bare rep per eval before any treatment cell; bare frac_met >= 0.85 = saturated (harden/drop, recorded here); 0.0 floor flagged; <2 discriminating evals = STOP and record

pilot.rule: 2 with-arm cells on eval 1 before the grid; >2x-estimate projection = STOP for re-approval

l2_protocol.judging: grok headline + claude divergence on identical cells; a verdict flip = exploratory, no KEEP/CUT promotion
l2_protocol.metrics: eval-clustered with-bare fraction-met delta + 95% CI (ADR 0019/0025); secondary: binary all-met + per-arm consistency (ADR 0065)

cost.estimate_note: notional estimate: L1 48-144 short trigger probes (~$2-8) + L2 36 tool-denied text cells (~$4-9) + judges (claude real ~$3-5, grok notional). Total estimate $9-20; ceiling_usd = 2x the upper estimate (ADR 0073), the stop-rule of record.

sample_rule.rule: every L2 cell record committed under prescreen-outputs/, pilot-outputs/, outputs/; every L1 variant report committed under trigger-results/; population appended post-run
